alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. The Codex Alimentarius Commission establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities, which are the maximum concentrations legally permitted to protect human health. The General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) is the primary reference.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance required. Food safety criteria define the acceptability of a product or a batch of food applicable to products placed on the market.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Listeria monocytogenes (shelf-life studies, sampling)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance required. Shelf-life studies (durability studies and challenge tests) and sampling procedures for Listeria monocytogenes in food processing areas and equipment must be conducted according to technical guidance and guidelines to comply with food safety criteria in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Production process hygiene
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance required. Process hygiene criteria indicate the correct functioning of the production process.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - General Halal food requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This is a mandatory technical regulation for general Halal food requirements.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements for packaged food
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This is a mandatory technical regulation for packaged food labels.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Mandatory shelf life periods
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This is a mandatory technical regulation for mandatory shelf life periods.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Product specifications for sports supplementary foods
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This is a mandatory technical regulation for sports supplementary foods.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - General requirements for Halal food
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification of general requirements for Halal food is required for products claiming to be Halal.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - Labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification of specific labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs is required as per this technical regulation.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - Mandatory expiration dates for food products
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification of specific mandatory expiration dates for food products is required as per this technical regulation.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification of specific microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients is required as per this technical regulation.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food Additive Safety Assessment
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Food additives must undergo safety assessment by SFA before being used in food sold in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - General Compliance for Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

alerts.title: Modified: Singapore Additive Limits - Permitted Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Verification required using SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool. New: Check official source. Only food additives assessed and permitted by SFA are allowed for use in food products.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Prohibition of Unpermitted Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Food products containing an additive that is not permitted must not be imported or sold.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Compliance with Contaminant Limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Food businesses must also comply with the Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Compliance with Tested Contaminant Limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. If laboratory test results indicate contaminants, they must be within specified limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - General Compliance for Contaminants
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents (contaminants) in food.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - General Contaminant Limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Contaminants (incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Prohibited Veterinary Drug Residues (Oestrogens)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Import or sale of meat and food derived from meat containing diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, or dienoestrol is prohibited.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Prohibition of Exceeding Contaminant Limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Food products with contaminants exceeding regulatory limits must not be imported or sold.

alerts.title: Modified: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Declaration of Ingredients and Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels. New: Check official source. For prepacked foods, all ingredients and additives used must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.

alerts.title: Modified: Taiwan Allergen List - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Mandatory declaration if present as ingredient or food additive. New: Mandatory declaration. Required if present as an ingredient or food additive.

alerts.title: Modified: Taiwan Allergen List - Sulphite as residue sulphur dioxide
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: 10 or more. New: 10 or more. Required if present at or above the specified limit.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Prohibited Foods
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Certain foods are prohibited from production, import, or sale. Specific lists of prohibited foods must be verified.

alerts.title: Added: United States Additive Limits - Delisted color additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not permitted. Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) are not permitted.

alerts.title: Added: United States Additive Limits - Prohibited substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not permitted. Substances listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) are prohibited from use in food.

alerts.title: Added: United States Allergen List - Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory declaration. The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Undeclared allergens (due to cross-contact)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not permitted. If a food product is found to inadvertently contain a food allergen due to cross-contact, it is considered misbranded or adulterated and subject to enforcement actions.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - "Gluten-free" claim
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must meet defined standard for gluten content. The FDA has a final rule defining "gluten-free" for food labeling, including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Allergen preventive controls and accurate labeling
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory compliance with 21 CFR part 117. The FDA's "Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food" rule (21 CFR part 117) requires facilities to implement allergen preventive controls to prevent cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Cochineal extract and carmine
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be declared on labels. Cochineal extract and carmine, derived from insects, must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products as allergenic substances.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be declared. The food source of all major food allergens must be identified on packaged food labels. This can be done in parentheses following the ingredient name (e.g., "lecithin (soy)") or immediately after/next to the ingredient list in a "contains" statement (e.g., "Contains wheat, milk, and soy."). Specific types of tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - FD&C Yellow No. 5
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be identified on labels. All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Sulfiting agents (e.g., sodium bisulfite)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: ≥10. Various sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food at a concentration of ≥10 parts per million total sulfites due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals.

alerts.title: Removed: China Additive Limits - Specific food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. New: Verification required. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth conditions for permitted food additives. Specific provisions are available in the searchable online database by food additive, functional class, and food category. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpretation.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. New: Adherence to Code of Practice required. A Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices. This document provides guidance for minimizing contamination in this food category.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (general)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and Toxins
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Food additives must be approved for use in the EU and comply with their conditions of use as listed in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008. Access to the Additives Database is required for verification.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Salmonella prevalence
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Special guarantees regarding Salmonella prevalence apply to trade of certain live animals and products from Sweden, Finland, and Norway. Other EU countries or regions can obtain similar guarantees if their control programmes are recognized as equivalent to those approved for Sweden and Finland, in accordance with Regulations (EC) No 853/2004 (foodstuffs) and 2160/2003 (animals).

alerts.title: Added: Japan Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required against official specifications and standards.. Specific limits and conditions for food additives are detailed in 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Agricultural Chemical Residues
alerts.details: New: Verification required against the Positive List System.. The 'Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food' establishes maximum residue limits.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (general)
alerts.details: New: Verification required against official specifications and standards.. General contaminant limits are expected to be covered by 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Radioactivity
alerts.details: New: Verification required against guidelines for handling contaminated food.. Guidelines exist for 'Handling of food contaminated by radioactivity'.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Nutrition Labeling
alerts.details: New: Verification required for specific nutrition labeling requirements.. Requirements for nutrition labeling are outlined under 'Food with Health Claims, Food for Special Dietary Uses, and Nutrition Labeling'.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Labelling Requirement - Source fetch / extraction issue
alerts.details: Old: Manual review required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Microbiology Limits - Microbiological standards
alerts.details: New: Verification required against official specifications and standards.. Microbiological standards for food are expected to be covered by 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Compliance with Additive Limits
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Different limits apply based on the additive and food type; verification is required using SFA's Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives.
